title: Green Plains Enters into Agreement with Freepoint Commodities to Monetize 45Z Tax Credits
content: Green Plains Inc. (NASDAQ: GPRE) has entered into an agreement with an affiliate of Freepoint Commodities LLC to sell Clean Fuel Production Credits, or 45Z tax credits, generated in 2025 under the Inflation Reduction Act, marking a significant step in monetizing its low-carbon intensity ethanol production strategy. The initial credits will come from Green Plainsâ€™ three Nebraska facilities, with some credits generated even before the launch of its carbon capture systems later this year, showcasing the companyâ€™s competitive carbon intensity (CI) profile. Additionally, Green Plains has signed a term sheet with Freepoint to monetize credits from three more facilities expected to qualify under 45Z during 2025, further broadening its clean fuel credit portfolio. CEO Chris Osowski highlighted that the deal aligns with the companyâ€™s strategy to unlock value from low-CI operations while advancing carbon capture through its Trailblazer CCS project. The agreement, which covers 2025 credits and may extend through 2029, will use a direct transfer mechanism supported by third-party emissions verification and tax insurance, providing enhanced transparency and reliability. Based on production expectations, Green Plains projects $40â€“$50 million in 45Z EBITDA net of discounts and expenses in 2025, with credits beginning to be recorded in Q3, strengthening both near-term value creation and long-term decarbonization goals.
author: Analysts Account
